title: Tool-use structured output for workflow agent steps that declare output.schema — in-process dispatch for schema-bound non-tool-using agents
body:
Workflow agent steps with output.schema or output.format: json dispatch via subprocess (dispatch.ts:162, spawn('pi', ['--mode', 'json', ...])). The LLM call happens inside pi-coding-agent, not in our code. We can't control the complete() call parameters. The subprocess agent receives a prompt that says 'respond with JSON only' and the model may or may not comply. step-agent.ts:205-241 validates post-hoc via JSON.parse(), failing the step on malformed output.

The mechanism for guaranteed structured output: the Anthropic API supports 'phantom tool' calls — define a Tool with the output schema as its TypeBox parameters, pass it in Context.tools, set toolChoice: { type: 'tool', name: '<name>' }. The LLM is forced to produce a ToolCall response whose arguments match the schema. The tool is never executed — it exists only as a schema constraint on the API response format. The API validates the arguments before returning them. This is what issue-026 implemented for monitor classify calls.

Two paths for workflow agents: (1) Agent steps that produce structured output WITHOUT iterative tool use (analyzers, classifiers, planners — agents whose role is reasoning, not file manipulation) could dispatch in-process via complete() with the phantom tool pattern. The agent spec has the model, compileAgentSpec() renders the prompt, output.schema converts to a TypeBox tool definition. No subprocess needed. (2) Agent steps that need iterative tool use (implementers, task-workers calling read/write/bash across multiple turns) must stay subprocess. Structured output enforcement there requires pi-coding-agent to support the phantom tool pattern when the agent spec declares output.schema — that's an upstream change outside this repo.

The agent spec could declare a dispatch mode: dispatch: 'in-process' for schema-bound reasoning agents, dispatch: 'subprocess' (default) for tool-using agents. Or infer it: if agent spec has no tools field, dispatch in-process; if it has tools, dispatch via subprocess.

Impacted components: step-agent.ts (branch dispatch path based on agent spec), dispatch.ts (unchanged for subprocess agents), types.ts AgentSpec/StepSpec (dispatch mode field), agent-spec.ts parseAgentYaml() (read dispatch field), workflow-sdk.ts agentContracts() (surface dispatch mode), VALIDATION_CHECKS (warn if agent declares tools + in-process dispatch), skill generator agent vocabulary table (show dispatch mode per agent). The phantom tool definition logic (schema → TypeBox Tool → toolChoice → ToolCall extraction) should be extracted from classifyViaAgent() in pi-behavior-monitors into a shared utility importable by both packages.
